message: Families in the school shelter have no flour left and children are sleeping without meals.
The water point is broken again and people are using unsafe water from the valley.
There are no lights near the latrines and women feel unsafe at night.
The clinic ran out of fever medicine and several children are sick.
New arrivals need plastic sheets before the next rain.
Food prices increased and the cash support is no longer enough for large families.
The road is blocked and the mobile health team has not reached the settlement.
Several children were separated during movement and families are asking for tracing support.
Soap and hygiene kits are finished in our block.
Pregnant women need transport to the health center.
The temporary shelters leak when it rains and blankets are wet.
People are scared to cross the checkpoint to register for assistance.
We received sorghum but there is not enough cooking oil for the families.
The latrines are full and children are getting stomach pain.
Older people need regular medicine for blood pressure.
The distribution list missed newly displaced households.
Families need more shade materials because the heat is severe.
There is harassment near the water queue and women ask for safer collection times.
Milk for infants is not available in the market.
The hand pump works only one hour a day and queues are very long.
The tent area is crowded and families need privacy partitions.
Malaria cases are increasing and there are not enough test kits.
Women asked for separate latrines and better lighting.
Bread is available but families cannot afford it after losing income.
The water truck did not arrive yesterday and people are sharing from one tank.
Children have no safe space and spend the day near the road.
A new group arrived last night and needs emergency food rations.
Many families lost documents during flight and need registration help.
Flood water damaged shelters and people are sleeping outside.
The health post has no clean delivery supplies.
